intro: The clearest difference between Tink and Didit here is transparency: Tink scores 2/5 on our transparency rating, Didit scores 5/5. Tink is European open banking platform providing bank account aggregation, payment initiation, and income verification APIs; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Didit is Free KYC verification API with document scanning, NFC chip reading, and facial biometric matching for identity checks; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Pick Tink for Fintech, Marketplace; pick Didit for Fintech, Marketplace. Tink is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Didit is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Honest trade-offs: Tink is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements, and Didit is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the KYC / KYB / AML API umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: Tink is for fintech, marketplace; Didit is for fintech, marketplace; decide based on scope fit.
verdict_a: Choose Tink if transparency rated 2/5 is acceptable and your use case is fintech, marketplace.
verdict_b: Choose Didit if transparency rated 5/5 fits your requirements and your use case is fintech, marketplace.

faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Tink uses subscription pricing with no free tier published. Didit uses subscription pricing starting at $666/mo.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Tink to Didit?
faq.a: Migration is workable but not trivial, since both sit around medium lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Both score 4/5 on developer experience in our data. Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Didit a good alternative to Tink?
faq.a: Didit sits in the same category as Tink, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Didit is best fit for fintech, marketplace. Note: Didit is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements.
